features.popup:
15BC128 — Mattingly

Breckinridge County · Petroglyph · Unknown (Woodland probable)

Location: North of Mattingly, south of Cloverport, western bank of Caney Creek, Mattingly Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: 63 bird tracks, 3 deer tracks, 3 rabbit tracks, pit-and-groove, human hand/footprints, hominy hole. Technique: Carved/incised. Setting: Sandstone rockshelter, 6m wide, open 3 sides, near Caney Creek. Elevation: 520 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1966; Fuller 1969a

15BC129 — Tar Springs

Breckinridge County · Petroglyph · Unknown

Location: Near Tar Springs and Mattingly Site, Mattingly Quad

Motifs: Spiral (clockwise double helix), 12 circular carvings, 17 bird tracks, concentric circles, circle-and-pit, deer track, curvilinear designs. Technique: Percussion (pecking only). Setting: Sandstone rockshelter, south-facing, 9.75m deep, 18m high. Elevation: 560 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1968; Fuller 1969a

15BC130 — North Fork of Rough River

Breckinridge County · Petroglyph · Late Woodland (ceramics, projectile points found - Hockensmi

Location: East of St. Anthony's School, south of Roff, Kingswood Quad, North Fork of Rough River north bank (approximate location)

Motifs: 14+ bird tracks, human hand print, 2 hominy holes. Technique: Not specified. Setting: Large south-facing rockshelter 27.5m wide, fallen roof piece, within Rough River Reservoir boundary. Elevation: 550 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1966; Hockensmith 1981

15BT40 — Baby Track Rock

Butler County · Petroglyph · Late Woodland (ceramics, projectile points - Hockensmith 198

Location: NNW of Mount Union Church, WNW of Cedar Grove School, Riverside Quad, 3mi N of Green-Barren confluence (approximate location)

Motifs: 5 human footprints (5-7.5cm x 10-25cm), 3 hominy holes. Technique: Carved on 45-degree slope. Setting: Sandstone rockshelter 13.2m wide, overlooking Welchs Creek, near Green-Barren River junction. Elevation: 600 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1966; Hockensmith 1981

15BT64 — Turkey Rock

Butler County · Petroglyph · Unknown

Location: Banks of Green River, Big Bend area, north of Allens Hill, Morgantown Quad, river miles 140-141 (approximate location)

Motifs: 6 quadrangular figures with undulating lines, 11+ bird tracks, 4 punctate groupings, triangular carving, serpentine line with oval punctates. Technique: Incised (percussion, not abrasion). Setting: Sandstone projection into Green River, frequently flooded, rock face at 30 degrees.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1967; Fuller 1969a, 1969b; Moore 1916

15BT65 — Reedyville

Butler County · Petroglyph · Unknown (within 15mi of 6 other rock art sites)

Location: SE of Reedyville, Reedyville Quad, near old Honakers Ferry, north side of Green River bend (approximate location)

Motifs: 5+ bird tracks, 4 human footprints, life-sized arm-and-hand print, pit-and-groove, circles with pits, cross-in-circle (sun circle), unique anthropomorph (arms outstretched above head on bisected circle). Technique: Pecking, abrasion, and incision. Setting: Lower Pennsylvanian sandstone (Caseyville Fm), triangular outcrop near Green River floodplain. Elevation: 500 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1966-1969; Fuller 1969a, 1969b

15CR60 — Carter Caves Pictograph

Carter County · Pictograph · Late Prehistoric possible (sun circle motif similarity, deba

Location: NE of Riggs Hill, Grahn Quad, NOT in Carter Caves proper (approximate location)

Motifs: Rectangular/oval outline enclosing equal-arm cross (55cm x 45cm), human footprint (0.6m). Only limestone canvas in Kentucky. One of only 2 pictograph sites in state.. Technique: Painted (pigment gone, algae-inhibition negative image). Setting: Base of limestone cliff face - unique substrate for Kentucky. Elevation: 740 ft.

Sources: Funkhouser & Webb 1932; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15CH200 — Pilot Rock

Christian County · Petroglyph · Unknown (no diagnostic artifacts)

Location: Atop Pilot Rock, Honey Grove Quad, SW of Flat Rock School, NE of Ebenezer Church, Christian/Todd county line

Motifs: 12+ hominy holes, 2-3 abrading areas, 3+ turkey tracks (1 possibly with red ocher), concentric circles, cupular pits. Technique: Rubbing alone. Setting: Small sandstone ledge near summit of Pilot Rock (prominent landmark, Big Clifty sandstone). Elevation: 966 ft.

Sources: Sanders 1977; Sanders & Maynard 1979; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15CY18 — Bar Creek

Clay County · Petroglyph · Unknown (questionable origin)

Location: North of Spurlock, south of Red Bird River, Barcreek Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: 3 bird tracks (center toes avg 9cm). Native American origin questioned due to unusual V-shaped incision.. Technique: Shallow with sharp V-shaped cross section (unusual). Setting: Sandstone rockshelter, petroglyphs on exterior western face (atypical placement). Elevation: 840 ft.

Sources: None known (first publication); Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15CY51 — Red Bird River

Clay County · Petroglyph · Unknown - Cherokee association legendary; pre-Columbian Euro

RELOCATED · NRHP listed

Location: NE bank of Red Bird River, along State Highway 66, 9.5mi upstream from Oneida

Motifs: Dense linear carvings on flat surface 1.6m x 6m. Stylistically unique. Stone fell Dec 7 1994 onto Hwy 66; moved to Manchester park. Original NRHP listing 1989, delisted 2003 after relocation.. Technique: Sharply incised straight lines (stylistically distinct). Setting: Vertical sandstone cliff face, 16ft above road, base of 60ft cliff along Hwy 66. Elevation: 1020 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1969b, 1970b; Funkhouser & Webb 1932; Wentworth 1969

15CY52 — Red Bird River Shelter

Clay County · Petroglyph · Unknown

Location: West bank of Red Bird River, south of Brutus Church, north of New Berry School, Big Creek Quad

Motifs: Linear incisions, several bird tracks (clustered near shelter opening, overlain by modern graffiti). Technique: Pounded, rubbed, and cut into sandstone. Setting: South-facing rockshelter, 2m mouth to 1.5m passage to 3m room, 5m total depth. Elevation: 950 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1969b, 1970b; Funkhouser & Webb 1932; Wentworth 1969

15CY53 — Fish Trap Rock

Clay County · Petroglyph

Location: West bank of Red Bird River, SE of New Berry School, Big Creek Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: 20-25 bird tracks (weathered/shallow, with unusual bars at right angles to spur). Technique: Pounded and rubbed. Setting: Large sandstone boulder 3.5m x 2-3m, smooth rounded surface, moved by bridge construction. Elevation: 950 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15CY265 — Laurel Branch

Clay County · Petroglyph

Location: On unnamed branch of Laurel Creek, east of Goose Creek, Bar Creek Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: Linear marks on diamond-shaped boulder (former rockshelter roof). Technique: Unknown. Setting: Small rockshelter under a waterfall, diamond-shaped boulder (0.85-1.35m sides, 62.5cm thick). Elevation: 1020 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15ED15 — Dismal Rock

Edmonson County · Petroglyph · Unknown (artifacts found, no cultural affiliation assigned)

Location: South of Kyrock Church, Bee Spring Quad, right bank of Nolin River (approximate location)

Motifs: 2 spirals (higher <20cm, lower ~10cm), 4+ bird tracks. Technique: Pecked (no rubbing traces). Setting: Cave with ledge and campsite, 60m W of Nolin River. Petroglyphs on roof fall against north wall.. Elevation: 550 ft.

Sources: Schwartz 1960; Fuller 1969a

15ED24 — Asphalt Rock Pictographs

Edmonson County · Pictograph · Mississippian or Fort Ancient (1000-1750 CE) - cordmarked po

Location: SW of New Liberty Church, Brownsville Quad, base of cliff line overlooking Green River Valley

Motifs: Horned/masked human figure (male, arms flexed 90 degrees above head, horn projections, cat-like mask/facial painting, deep red ochre pigment); large circle (41cm) with internal geometric zones; hourglass figure (15x27cm); concentric lines; small human head/torso; birdlike figure. One of only 2 pictograph sites in Kentucky.. Technique: Painted - red ochre (confirmed ancient by Kingery 1982 pigment analysis); black from carbonaceous source. Setting: Middle of 3 rockshelters at cliff base beneath plateau overlooking Green River Valley. 45m long, 9m deep, 4.5m high, faces SW.. Elevation: 550 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1969; Fuller 1969a; Kingery 1982 (pigment analysis)

15ED96 — Little Fairdale

Edmonson County · Petroglyph

Location: SW of Nolin River, north of Cove Hollow Church, Nolin Reservoir Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: 2 bird tracks (axes point toward rockshelter/bedrock mortar areas 180m away on bluffs over Nolin River). Technique: Unknown. Setting: Sandstone boulder in open field, 0.6m diameter, flush with ground. Elevation: 660 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1966

15EL3 — Big Gimlet

Elliott County · Petroglyph

Location: NE side of Big Gimlet Creek, west of Access, north of Grundy Church, Bruin Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: 7+ bird tracks (close together, some overlapping), rabbit tracks, ovular holes of uncertain origin. Technique: Pecked. Setting: Enormous rockshelter - 93m across opening, 42m deep. Petroglyphs on roof fall 4.2m south of overhang lip.. Elevation: 800 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15ES26 — Sparks Indian Rock House

Estill County · Petroglyph · Unknown (complex multi-period palimpsest)

NRHP listed

Location: SE of Double Oak Cemetery, Leighton Quad, near Granny Richards Springs School (approximate location)

Motifs: Across 5 boulders: 3 skeletal heads (broad grooves, gouged eyes/mouths), rayed circle, 2 human feet + hand print, vulva form (confirmed NA per Swauger 1974, parallels in WV), human stick figure, rayed head (original NA, rays added later with metal), small footprint, 5 pits in circle. Complex palimpsest of NA and Euro-American modifications.. Technique: Pounded, pecked, rubbed (varies by boulder). Setting: Rockshelter with 5 carved boulders in eastern half. Elevation: 1200 ft.

Sources: NRHP listed (Statewide significance). Swauger 1974 (vulva form parallels in WV).; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15ES27 — Ashley

Estill County · Petroglyph

NRHP listed

Location: North of Mountain Spring School, NE of Watson School, Cob Hill Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: Panel of 6 bird tracks (base of south wall, randomly distributed). Technique: Unknown. Setting: North-facing rockshelter, 6m high, 16.2m wide at entrance, 12.2m deep. Elevation: 1200 ft.

Sources: NRHP listed 1989 (Statewide significance).; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15ES59 — Sparks II

Estill County · Petroglyph

Location: Ridgetop saddle near Sparks Indian Rock House, Leighton Quad

Motifs: 2+ human footprints (one nearly obliterated, second outlined with more recent circle). Technique: Pecked. Setting: Exposed rock outcrop on ridgetop saddle near Sparks Indian Rock House. Elevation: 1310 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15GY65 — Crow Hollow

Grayson County · Petroglyph

Location: NE of West Clifty, south of Goodman School, Big Clifty Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: Rabbit tracks, raccoon paw prints (northerly boulder); bird track with star-like abrasions (southerly boulder). Bedrock mortar present.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Rockshelter with two boulder sections bearing petroglyphs. Elevation: 600 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15GY66 — Saltsman Branch

Grayson County · Petroglyph

Location: South of Moutardier, SE of McGrew Church, Bee Spring Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: 12 bird tracks, 3 deer tracks, isolated bird track W of main group. Abrading area for tool sharpening. Vehicle traffic erosion degrading clarity.. Technique: Pecked and rubbed. Setting: Sandstone rock outcrop in roadbed along ridge saddle, grass-covered, vehicle-damaged. Elevation: 650 ft.

Sources: Coy 1991, 1993, 1994; Weller 1927

15GY67 — Saltsman Branch Shelter

Grayson County · Petroglyph

Location: NE of BM 700, west of BM 797, Nolin Reservoir Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: 7 bird tracks (entrance area), abrading area for tool sharpening (west NW wall), 7 linear incisions, additional bird track (toes oriented outward). Technique: Pecked and rubbed. Setting: Small south-facing sandstone rockshelter, 90m wide, 30m deep, 15m high. Elevation: 650 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15HA114 — Jeffry Cliff

Hancock County · Petroglyph

Location: North of Sunny Corner, Cloverport Quad, Hancock County (approximate location)

Motifs: 3 clusters: rounded square with U-shaped elements + scroll/S-shaped design; 6 straight lines pecked into floor rock; 3 straight lines, eroded semicircle, bedrock mortar 8cm diameter.. Technique: Pecked. Setting: Large NW-facing rockshelter with semicircular floor plan. Elevation: 550 ft.

Sources: Turnbow et al. 1980; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15JA160 — Daugherty Bear Track

Jackson County · Petroglyph

Location: West of Dry Fork School, SE of Corinth Church, Johnetta Quad, Jackson County (approximate location)

Motifs: 21 bear paw prints, 1 small + 2 large circles (with center holes, 4 paws associated), zig-zag linear design (similar to stick-figure animals at Indian God Rock, PA). Circles enclose 4 tracks each. Compass-point center holes in circles.. Technique: Pounding and rubbing. Setting: Sandstone outcrop on side of ravine, facing west, ~30m above freshwater spring. Petroglyph area 3.3 x 5.1m.. Elevation: 1200 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15JA161 — Brushy Ridge

Jackson County · Petroglyph

Location: North of Sparks School, McKee Quad, Jackson County (approximate location)

Motifs: Bird tracks (line of 5 on rear wall + 1 single higher), animal tracks (2 complete + 1 partial, possibly paw prints), arc of dots (possibly unfinished paw carving). Technique: Rubbed (very sharp lines, possibly with stone celt). Setting: Small sandstone rockshelter, 4.5 x 6m, 2.25m high. Elevation: 1480 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15JA234 — William Gay

Jackson County · Petroglyph

Location: Small knoll along SE-trending ridge, McKee Quad, Jackson County (approximate location)

Motifs: 9 bear tracks, 2 human footprints, 2 footprints combining human and bear characteristics, 1 deer track. Technique: Carved/incised. Setting: Sandstone outcrop near SE end of knoll along ridge separating Middle Fork and Lime Kiln drainages. Elevation: 1340 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15JA235 — Christmas Eve

Jackson County · Petroglyph

Location: West of Cavanaugh School, Sandgap Quad, Jackson County (approximate location)

Motifs: ~25 split-hoof animal tracks of various shapes. Traces of former nitre mining operations.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Sandstone boulder (~90cm diameter) in large rockshelter (18m across, 9m high, 12-18m deep). Elevation: 1400 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15JA355 — Peter Cave Branch

Jackson County · Petroglyph · Late Woodland (disturbed deposits identified by USFS)

Location: SE of Flat Top Church on Peter Cave Branch, Parrot Quad, Jackson County (approximate location)

Motifs: 2 human footprints, 1 bear track, 8+ bird tracks, 3 small tracks (mink or squirrel?), numerous abrading marks, abraded area resembling inverted comma. Many historic initials.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Large rockshelter with many grottoes. Disturbed Late Woodland archaeological deposits identified by USFS archaeologist Cecil Ison.. Elevation: 1020 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15LE55 — Big Turtle Shelter

Lee County · Petroglyph · 310 BC (O'Steen, Gremillion & Ledbetter 1991)

Location: South of Mt. Paran, Zachariah Quad, Lee County (approximate location)

Motifs: 2 sets of concentric circles (10 and 15cm diameter, severely weathered). Possibly cultural linear markings at one end. 3 bedrock mortars on separate rock. Dated to 310 BC.. Technique: Abraded. Setting: Large slab resting against ledge on rockshelter roof. Elevation: 1080 ft.

Sources: O'Steen, Gremillion & Ledbetter 1991; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15LE57 — Big Sinking Creek Turtle Rock

Lee County · Petroglyph

Location: NW of Zoe, north of Mt. Olive, Zachariah Quad, Lee County (approximate location)

Motifs: 7+ bird tracks (4 in line pointing east, 2 pointing N/SE, 1 isolated pointing SE), turtle (30cm head to tail, pointing ESE). Historic initials.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Sandstone outcrop on south-facing ridge north of Big Sandy Creek, adjacent to oil road.. Elevation: 1040 ft.

Sources: Knudsen & Simpson 1982; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15LE110 — Cave Fork Hill

Lee County · Petroglyph · Hominy hole undoubtedly prehistoric per Funkhouser & Webb

RELOCATED · ORIGINAL BOULDER LOST

Location: SW of Hopewell Church, SE of cemetery at Fixer, Zachariah Quad, Lee County (approximate location)

Motifs: Human footprints and bear tracks (on lost boulder). 2 bear tracks remain on roof fall in rockshelter. Original boulder removed to University of Kentucky - current location unknown, LOST.. Technique: Carved (possibly with blunt-pointed chisel or flint chisels). Setting: Rockshelter ~30ft wide, 40ft deep, formed by fallen cliff stones.. Elevation: 1120 ft.

Sources: Funkhouser & Webb 1929; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15LE111 — Perdue

Lee County · Petroglyph

Location: SE of Mt. Paran School, NW of cemetery at Fixer, Zachariah Quad, Lee County (approximate location)

Motifs: Bird track + unique curvilinear figure resembling insect (possibly dragonfly larva per Swauger). Insect motifs extremely rare in rock art.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Sandstone rockshelter 15m long, 5m high. Petroglyphs on tilted 45-degree slab.. Elevation: 1120 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15LE112 — Bear Track

Lee County · Petroglyph

Location: NE of Bear Track Lookout Tower, SE of Sandfield School, Cobhill Quad, Lee County (approximate location)

Motifs: Single bear track surrounded by circle. Design similar to Daugherty Bear Track (15JA160).. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Sandstone outcrop at head of Town Hollow. Elevation: 1080 ft.

Sources: Fuller 1969a

15LE113 — Old Landing

Lee County · Petroglyph

Location: South of BM 646, SW of Sandfield School, Cobhill Quad, Lee County (approximate location)

Motifs: Several series of vertical lines in roughly parallel rows on south face of cliff. Interpretive debate: calendrical marks, tally marks, counting coup, tool sharpening, or Old World records (unlikely).. Technique: Narrow cuts and wider cuts. Setting: South face of cliff, lines split by large cleft into west main group and east small cluster. Elevation: 1100 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15LE120 — Fincastle

Lee County · Petroglyph

Location: NW of Bethlehem Church, SW of Fincastle Church, Zachariah Quad, Lee County (approximate location)

Motifs: 6-7 bear tracks, bird track, unusual four-footed tailed animal with long neck and upright pointed ears (body transected by 2 broad lines beneath belly - bear track perched on its back). Swauger: may symbolize animal or man possessed of sacred power.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Sandstone rockshelter 80m entrance, 7m deep, 25m high. Petroglyphs on boulder near drip line.. Elevation: 800 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15LE215 — Little Sinking

Lee County · Petroglyph

Location: NE of Mt. Paran School, overlooking Little Sinking Creek, Zachariah Quad, Lee County (approximate location)

Motifs: At least 1 deer track and 1 bird track (severely weathered). Possible remnants of human footprints or bear tracks.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Sandstone point overlooking Little Sinking Creek. Elevation: 1200 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15LV160 — Mantle Rock

Livingston County · Petroglyph · Historic (probably) - Trail of Tears era (1838-39) or earlie

Trail of Tears National Historic Trail

Location: South of Cave Spring Church, Golconda Quad, Livingston County. Mantle Rock Nature Preserve (TNC).

Motifs: Fish, human figure, turkey. Trail of Tears site: 10,000+ Cherokee passed through winter 1838-39. Peter Hildebrand Detachment (~1766 people) camped 2 weeks waiting for frozen Ohio River to thaw. Now 367-acre Nature Conservancy preserve, certified Trail of Tears National Historic Trail site (NPS 2004).. Technique: Pecked with sharp metallic tool (probably historic date). Setting: Small rockshelter, sandstone slab. 30ft high natural sandstone bridge spanning 188ft - largest freestanding arch east of Mississippi.. Elevation: 400 ft.

Sources: NPS Trail of Tears documentation; TNC preserve records; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15MA197 — Burnt Ridge

Madison County · Petroglyph

Location: NE of Macedonia Church, Berea Quad, Madison County (approximate location)

Motifs: 3 human stick figures (one possibly male with penis or lizard with tail), raptorial bird with widespread wings, chevrons. Technique: Pit-and-groove. Setting: Small sandstone rockshelter 9m long, 4.5m deep, 4.5m high, faces west. Elevation: 1400 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15MD308 — Payneville

Meade County · Petroglyph

Location: West of Sirocco, east of Payneville, Irvington Quad, Meade County (approximate location)

Motifs: Many bird tracks on flat horizontal surface (1.8-2.4m). Popular picnic spot - many historic/modern initials.. Technique: Carved. Setting: Outcropping of sandstone forming small series of ledges on wooded hillside. Elevation: 720 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1966

15MF160 — Indian Stairway

Menifee County · Petroglyph

Location: NW of Tower Rock, NE of Chimney Top Rock, Pomeroyton Quad, Menifee County (approximate location)

Motifs: 4 concentric circles carved into sandstone slab on rockshelter floor. Technique: Carved/abraded. Setting: Large sandstone rockshelter facing east. Elevation: 1000 ft.

Sources: Wyss & Wyss 1977; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15MF178 — Stone Foot Shelter

Menifee County · Petroglyph · Late Archaic / Early Woodland (diagnostic artifacts)

Location: Very near Menifee-Powell County line, NW of Short Creek, Frenchburg Quad (approximate location)

Motifs: On 2 boulders: bedrock mortar + at least 2 human footprints; 2-3 human footprints, 2 deer tracks, figure-eight geometric design. Dense prehistoric artifacts; evidence of severe looting.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Large east-facing rockshelter, 45m across, 8m deep. Near Menifee-Powell County line.. Elevation: 1300 ft.

Sources: Jobe, Stafford & Boisvert 1980; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15MF199 — Bells Falls

Menifee County · Petroglyph

Location: SW of juncture of Powell Branch with Gladie Creek, Pomeroyton Quad, Menifee County. Within Daniel Boone NF. (approximate location)

Motifs: Row of straight lines, 1-2 possible + 2 definite split-hoof animal tracks, bird track. Within Daniel Boone National Forest.. Technique: Pecked and ground. Setting: Rockshelter with boulder near drip line. Elevation: 1200 ft.

Sources: Knudsen 1980; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15MF353 — Spratts

Menifee County · Petroglyph

Location: SE of Natural Arch, NW of Kendrick Ridge Church, Scranton Quad, Menifee County (approximate location)

Motifs: Abstract and curvilinear designs with at least 2 bird tracks (main group, linear arrangement on upper face of boulder 1.8 x 1.7m).. Technique: Unknown. Setting: East-facing sandstone rockshelter with shallow overhang. Boulder in north-central portion.. Elevation: 1100 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15MF354 — Skidmore

Menifee County · Petroglyph

Location: South of Hawkins Branch School, SW of Leatherwood School, Frenchburg Quad. (approximate location)

Motifs: Possible plant motif (multiple-branched pecked petroglyph with terminal clusters - plant motifs very unusual in Eastern US rock art). Several bird tracks lacking hind toes. Also known as Short Creek Site.. Technique: Pecked. Setting: Large south-facing rockshelter, 30m wide, 7.4-8.5m deep. 2 large boulders in central portion.. Elevation: 1380 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1971

15PO23 — Martin Fork

Powell County · Petroglyph

Location: South of juncture of Martin Fork and Grays Branch of Red River, Slade Quad, Powell County (approximate location)

Motifs: 3+ sets of large hoof tracks interpreted as elk (not deer - much larger). 2 concentric rings + elk track. Nearly obliterated large bird track. USFS has placed fence around site.. Technique: Pecked then smoothed by abrasion. Setting: 2 boulders in large sandstone rockshelter with semicircular cliff face. 60m long, 18m high, 12m deep, facing 270 degrees.. Elevation: 1150 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1971; Weinland & Sanders 1977

15PO25 — High Rock

Powell County · Petroglyph

RELOCATED

Location: North of Rogers Chapel, SE of Cat Creek Church, Stanton Quad, Powell County

Motifs: Complex geometric and linear designs - most unusual and complicated beyond the normal designs found in the northeastern United States (Swauger). Boulder moved to Red River Museum in Clay City for protection.. Technique: Pecked with some smoothing by abrasion. Setting: Sandstone rockshelter 47m long, 9.6m deep, 15-18m high, facing SSW.. Elevation: 1350 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1971; Weinland & Sanders 1977

15PO106 — State Rock

Powell County · Petroglyph

Location: At State Rock, prominently marked on Stanton Quad, Powell County

Motifs: Three-toed turkey tracks at top of naturally formed steps. 2 tracks definitely Native American, 1 possible track obscured by superimposed V initial.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: At State Rock, prominently marked on Stanton Quad. Elevation: 1280 ft.

Sources: Weinland & Sanders 1977; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15PO107 — McKinney Bluff

Powell County · Petroglyph

Location: SW of juncture of Short Creek and Red River, NW of Middle Fork Church, Slade Quad, Powell County (approximate location)

Motifs: 4 human-like footprints carved on large boulder (1.2 x 1.4m). Footprints equivalent size (14cm long, 5cm wide). One pair close together and perpendicular.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Rockshelter on east slope of McKinney Cliff. Elevation: 1080 ft.

Sources: Weinland & Sanders 1977; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15PO108 — Amburgy Hollow

Powell County · Petroglyph

DESTROYED

Location: NW of Courthouse Rock, east of juncture of Spaas Creek and Red River, Slade Quad, Powell County (approximate location)

Motifs: Series of cut lines on SE wall. Deer tracks and other designs formerly on rear wall NOW DESTROYED - apparently removed by someone attempting to take them.. Technique: Cut lines. Setting: Sandstone rockshelter 10.4m wide, 7.6m deep, 4m high. Elevation: 1100 ft.

Sources: Coy & Fuller 1971; Weinland & Sanders 1977

15PO154 — Whites

Powell County · Petroglyph

Location: Near Estill County line, SW of Rogers Chapel, Stanton Quad, Powell County. (approximate location)

Motifs: Circular and wavy line elements. Chevron design on ledge 2m north of main boulder. Also known as Fayes Petroglyph Site.. Technique: Pounded and rubbed. Setting: East-facing sandstone rockshelter with large boulder (1.4m x 1m x 32cm). Near Estill County line.. Elevation: 1100 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15PO155 — Nada Tunnel 1

Powell County · Petroglyph · Woodland period (1000 BCE - 1000 CE)

NRHP listed

Location: SW of juncture of Schoolhouse Branch and Red River, Slade Quad, Powell County (approximate location)

Motifs: Narrow lines forming V-shapes on rear wall of shallow cave. One set enclosed forming triangular element. Chevron patterns similar to Whites, 7.5 miles away. NRHP listed 1992.. Elevation: 1000 ft.

Sources: NRHP 1992.; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15PO156 — Nada Tunnel 2

Powell County · Petroglyph · Woodland period (1000 BCE - 1000 CE)

NRHP listed

Location: SW of juncture of Schoolhouse Branch and Red River, Slade Quad, Powell County (approximate location)

Motifs: 3 concentric circles (identified as Native American) on north-slanting rock facing outward. NRHP listed 1992.. Setting: Large NW-facing sandstone rockshelter at head of Moreland Branch. Elevation: 1220 ft.

Sources: NRHP 1992.; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15PO158 — Branham Ridge

Powell County · Petroglyph

Location: SE of Vaughns Mill, Clay City Quad, Powell County. (approximate location)

Motifs: Numerous bird tracks oriented in all directions on sandstone outcrop on top of ridge. Also known as Buckner Ridge and Hardwicke Branch.. Technique: Carved. Setting: Sandstone outcrop on ridge top. Elevation: 1300 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15PO159 — Knox

Powell County · Petroglyph

Location: SE of Cat Creek Church, east of State Rock, Stanton Quad, Powell County (approximate location)

Motifs: 5 bird tracks and 2 cloven hoof tracks carved on 30x40cm panel on west wall of small cave.. Technique: Carved. Setting: Small cave, 3m wide, 10m deep, facing north above South Fork of Red River. Elevation: 1135 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15PO160 — Steven DeHart

Powell County · Petroglyph · Woodland or late prehistoric (ceramics, corn cobs in associa

Location: North of Slade Interchange, east of Middle Fork Church, Slade Quad, Powell County (approximate location)

Motifs: Linear elements interpreted as abrading lines and paw marks. DeHart Rockshelter assemblage included ceramics, corn cobs, shell spoons, gourd fragments.. Technique: Unknown. Setting: Small rockshelter facing SW, 14m wide, 5m deep, 3-5m high.. Elevation: 1000 ft.

Sources: Funkhouser & Webb 1930, 1932; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15PO281 — Ledford Hollow

Powell County · Petroglyph

Location: Powell County (approximate location)

Motifs: 2 well-executed human footprints. Unusual: both have only 4 toes and well-defined arches.. Technique: Pecked and abraded. Setting: South-facing rockshelter, 23.8m wide, 20m high, 17m deep.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15RK49 — Loman Hill

Rockcastle County · Petroglyph

DESTROYED

Location: South of Dicycope Chapel, NW of Climax Church, Johnetta Quad, Rockcastle County (approximate location)

Motifs: Originally 12 foot-prints. Burroughs (1938) believed prints were 250 million years old; Bushnell (Smithsonian) said undoubtedly Indian carvings. DESTROYED between 1970-1972.. Technique: Carved. Setting: Sandstone ledge outcropping on top of ridge. SITE DESTROYED.. Elevation: 1440 ft.

Sources: Coy 1991, 1993; Burroughs 1938a, 1938b, 1940

15RU42 — Jabez

Russell County · Petroglyph

Location: SE of Lake View Church, north of Coleman Church, Jabez Quad, Russell County (approximate location)

Motifs: 8 human footprints on north wall (toes vary 3-5). 10 additional footprints outside on weather-exposed wall. Altogether different style from other KY sites - more elongated and stylized. Lake Cumberland Petroglyph.. Technique: Pounded and pecked (Swauger style). Setting: Narrow cave/rockshelter near top of bluff overlooking Cumberland River and Cumberland Lake. Elevation: 980 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15WO19 — Caesar Hurst Shelter

Wolfe County · Petroglyph

Location: SW of Grannis, east of Big Andy Church, Campton Quad, Wolfe County (approximate location)

Motifs: Originally 2 bird tracks and arrow. Later: 4 turkey tracks + bow and arrow. Hominy hole. Bow considered a later non-Native addition.. Technique: Unknown (site modified by non-Native carvers). Setting: Enormous rockshelter - 60m wide, 18m deep, at least 21m high.. Elevation: 1020 ft.

Sources: Funkhouser & Webb 1932; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15WO26 — Trinity Shelter

Wolfe County · Petroglyph

Location: South of Tower Rock, Pomeroyton Quad, Wolfe County (approximate location)

Motifs: Footprint, possible vulva form, possible human stick figure, V-shaped elements (possible deer tracks). Second vulva form in Kentucky if confirmed.. Technique: Carved. Setting: Central rockshelter in series of 3 connected shelters. 50m deep. South of Tower Rock.. Elevation: 1020 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997

15WO119 — Seventeen Seventeen

Wolfe County · Petroglyph

Location: NE of juncture of Lower Devil Creek and Panther Branch, Campton Quad, Wolfe County (approximate location)

Motifs: 5-6 bird tracks. Historic carving of 1717 - if a date, predates first documented Euro-American visit to Kentucky (Dr. Thomas Walker, 1750) by 33 years.. Technique: Incised. Setting: Sandstone outcrop at base of cliff line. Elevation: 1000 ft.

Sources: None known; Coy, Fuller, Meadows & Swauger 1997
